info.title: Official Anaplan Collection
info.description: The Anaplan Integration API v2 provides REST endpoints for programmatic access to Anaplan resources: workspaces, models, files, imports, exports, processes, actions, lists, and modules. Use this API to automate data movement and model operations.

servers.description: US1, US2, US5, US7, EU1, EU2, EU4, AP1

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Retrieves information about all models in the user's default tenant.

parameters.name: modelDetails
parameters.description: When true, includes available model memory usage in bytes in the response.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort results. `+field` ascending, `-field` descending; prefix required and field name validated (bad or unknown field returns 400). Comma-separate to sort by multiple columns. Sortable fields: name, activeState.

200.description: All models in the user's default tenant.

get.description: Minimum role: Standard User.

get.summary: List the actions in the model as a JSON array
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Model-direct form of `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/actions`. Returns the same response shape without requiring a workspace ID. Lists delete actions available for the specified model.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort results. `+field` or unprefixed ascending, `-field` descending; comma-separate for multiple columns. `name` and `id` reorder results. Field name not validated — unknown fields return 200 and fall back to default order. Example: `+name`, `-id`.

get.description: Minimum role: Workspace Administrator.

put.description:
Minimum role: Workspace Administrator. Use this call to change or reset the current period in Anaplan. When this call contains a specified date (for example, 2020-05-20), the API determines what period range contains the specified data and sets that range as the current period. When the date value is set to blank in this call, the API resets the current period to blank. **Warning**: A change to your time settings is a potentially destructive action and may lead to data loss. The new range of time periods may not include previous time periods. Any data in the removed time periods is deleted from the model. **Notes**: * Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response ### Request body | **Body** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `{date}` |

* Required
* Type: String
* Description: The date to use to set, or reset, the current period. The date format is YYYY-MM-DD.
If the `date` parameter is left blank (for example, `/currentperiod?date=`) the current period resets.
If the date is not provided in the YYYY-MM-DD format, the call returns a `400 Bad Request` response with this message: `Invalid ISO date format '{date}'. Date should match format YYYY-MM-DD`
If this value falls outside of a valid range, the call returns a `400 Bad Request` response with this message: `Specified date '{date}' is out of timescale range {start of timescale} - {end of timescale}`.
* Example: 2010-05-20

|

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date used to set the current period (YYYY-MM-DD). Leave blank (?date=) to reset the current period. Accepted as either a query parameter or a request body field, but not both simultaneously. Returns 400 if the format is invalid or the date falls outside the model timescale.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

400.description: Bad request. Occurs when: (1) the date is not in YYYY-MM-DD format — "Invalid ISO date format '{date}'. Date should match format YYYY-MM-DD"; (2) the date falls outside the model timescale — "Specified date '{date}' is out of timescale range {start} - {end}"; (3) date is supplied as both a query parameter and a request body field simultaneously.

get.summary: Model-direct form: Retrieve all items in a dimension
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Alternate URL form — response is equivalent to `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items`.

Use this call to retrieve the IDs, codes, and names for items in a specified dimension. The dimension must be a list, a list subset, a line item subset, or the Users dimension.

Calls at model-level do not support the Time dimension. The calls return a `400` response. To avoid this, use the view dimension instead.

**Notes**:

*   This call is on the model-level. It returns all dimension items as it does not have view-level hiding, filtering, or [Selective Access](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Users/Selective_Access.html). For the view-level endpoint, see Retrieve selected items in a dimension.
*   If codes have not been set for an item, then the response excludes the `code` field.
*   This call only supports the retrieval of dimensions and items from a dimension that contains a maximum of 1,000,000 items.  
    The call returns a `400` response for any more than that number of items.
*   Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response.

400.description: Bad request — check the request parameters and body.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

post.summary: Model-direct form: Lookup dimension items by name or code
post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Model-direct alternate URL form — identical behaviour to `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items` POST.

Retrieve the items from a dimension that match one of a list of names or a list or codes.

** Notes: **

*   This call does not return results for names or codes for which an item does not exist.
*   If the given workspace, model, or dimension do not exist, this call returns a `404` code.
*   If the dimension does not support codes and codes are provided, this call returns a `400` code.
*   In the future, use of this call may be rate limited.

### Request body

| **JSON Element** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| `names` | The case-insensitive strings that match the name of a returned item. |
| `codes` | The case-insensitive strings that match the code of a returned item. |

Note that the `names`, `codes`, or both fields in the request body must contain a non-empty value. If the request body does not include at least one of these fields, the response returns an error.

404.description: Not found — the referenced resource does not exist.

400.description: Bad request — check the request parameters and body.

get.summary: List the available export definitions as a JSON array
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Model-direct form of `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/exports`. Returns the same response shape without requiring a workspace ID. Lists export definitions available for the specified model.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort results. `+field` or unprefixed ascending, `-field` descending; comma-separate for multiple columns. `name` and `id` reorder results. Field name not validated — unknown fields return 200 and fall back to default order. Example: `+name`, `-id`.

get.summary: Get the metadata for an export definition
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Model-direct form of `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/exports/{exportId}`. Returns the same `exportMetadata` response shape without requiring a workspace ID.

Minimum role: Standard User.

To upload a file, you need its ID in Anaplan:

*   `id` is the ID that you need to upload the file.
*   `name` is the filename of the file.
*   `chunkCount` is the number of chunks in the file.
*   `delimiter` is double-quotes for columns and fields. For example, "Versions","Opportunities"," "Version 1","Opportunity 0001".
*   `encoding` determines the way in which bytes in a file map to Unicode character code points. See [Common Character Encodings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Character_encoding#Common_character_encodings) on Wikipedia.
*   `firstDataRow` is the first row after the row containing column names.
*   `format` is text, as opposed to a binary stream.
*   `headerRow` is the row that contains column names.
*   `separator` is the token that separates fields. This is typically a comma, for example, "Versions","Opportunities"," "Version 1","Opportunity 0001".

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Accept:application/json`

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort results. `+field` or unprefixed ascending, `-field` descending; comma-separate for multiple columns. `name` and `id` reorder results. Field name not validated — unknown fields return 200 and fall back to default order. Example: `+name`, `-id`.

200.description: Files in the model, each with the ID needed to upload.

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Model-direct equivalent of `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/files/{fileId}/chunks`. Returns the same response shape: a `chunks` array of `{id, name}` objects (key omitted entirely when `chunkCount` is 0).

200.description: Chunk IDs and names for the file.

get.summary: Get a list of import actions
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Model-direct form of `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/imports`. Returns the same response shape without requiring a workspace ID. Lists import definitions available for the specified model.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort results. `+field` or unprefixed ascending, `-field` descending; comma-separate for multiple columns. `name` and `id` reorder results. Field name not validated — unknown fields return 200 and fall back to default order. Example: `+name`, `-id`.

get.summary: Get the metadata for an import definition
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Model-direct form of `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/imports/{importId}`. Returns the same `importMetadata` response shape without requiring a workspace ID.

get.summary: Retrieve all line items in a model
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve identifiers of all line items in a model.

**Notes**:

*   The returned line items are restricted just to those in modules to which the model role of the user has Read access.
*   The response returns Items within a [flat list](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Dimensions/Lists.html) (no hierarchy).
*   This returns all the line items in the model. For line items in a specific module see Retrieve Line Items For a Module.
*   Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response

200.description: All line items in the model.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

get.summary: Retrieve dimensions IDs for a line item
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve the IDs of dimensions that define a line item.

For reference, see Retrieve dimension items for a line item.

Notes:

*   This returns a list of all the dimensions that apply to that line item.

200.description: Dimensions that define the line item

get.summary: Retrieve dimension items for a line item
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve IDs, codes, and names for dimension items that apply for the specified line item.

**Notes**:

*   This call works with any dimension type.
*   Your requesting model role must allow read access to the module the line item belongs to. Otherwise, this call returns a `404 Not Found` HTTP status.
*   The items in the response are ordered as listed in the Anaplan model.
*   The response returns items within a [flat list](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Dimensions/Lists.html) (no hierarchy).
*   This returns dimension items that apply to a line item. For the view-level call, see Retrieve selected items in a dimension and for the model-level call, see Retrieve all items in a dimension.
*   This call only supports the retrieval of dimension items where the dimension contains a maximum of 1,000,000 items. If the specified dimension contains more than 1,000,000 dimension items, then the call returns a 400 Bad Request HTTP status, instead of a subset of the dimension items.
*   Providing a non-existent Line Item ID or Dimension ID returns a 404 Not Found HTTP status.
*   If the request provides an invalid line item ID or dimension ID this call returns a `400 Bad Request` HTTP status.
*   In the future, use of this call may be rate limited.

200.description: Items of the dimension for the line item.

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Alternate URL form — response is equivalent to `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/lists`.

Use this call to retrieve lists for a specified workspace and model.

Minimum role: Standard User.

Alternate URL form — response is equivalent to `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/lists/{listId}`.

Use this call to retrieve metadata for a specified list. Field-level descriptions are in the response schema (ListMetadata). Metadata entries that are not set for a list (for example, no parent or no top level item) are omitted from the response.

**Notes**:

*   Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

get.description:
Minimum role: Workspace Administrator.

Model-direct equivalent of `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/modelCalendar`. Returns the same response shape: a `modelCalendar` object containing the current fiscal year with start and end dates (empty object when the calendar type has no fiscal year, e.g., Weeks General).

put.description:
Minimum role: Workspace Administrator.

Use this call to update the current fiscal year for the model calendar. The year input value has to be a valid fiscal year supported by the model calendar in Anaplan. If successful, this call returns the updated fiscal year with start and end date associated with current model calendar type. This call returns a `400 bad request` message if the input value is out of range or model calendar type does not have an available fiscal year (for example, Weeks General).

year.description: Fiscal year to set as current. Must be a valid fiscal year for the model's calendar type (for example, FY21).

200.description: The updated fiscal year with its start and end dates.

get.summary: Retrieve IDs and names for all modules in a model
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve the IDs and names of all modules for a specified model.

**Notes**:

*   Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response

200.description: IDs and names of all modules in the model.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

post.summary: Write cell data by coordinate to module
post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to set cell values in a module.

Ensure the request body is an array of `CellWrite` objects. Each object is for an updated individual cell and its full set of dimensions.

The response contains a summary of the number of cells updated successfully and the details of why some cells that could not be updated.

**Notes:**

*   Ensure the user has write access enabled.
*   Ensure the request includes all of the required fields.
*   Ensure the request has a `value` field that is either a string, number or boolean and is compatible with the line item format/data type.
*   If one cell cannot update, the other cells in the request still continue to update.
*   Workspace administrators can import data into any cell (except aggregate cells, see below), including read-only and invisible cells.
*   You can update a maximum of 1000 cells per request. For larger scale writes we recommend you use the import API.
*   Write cell data does not apply to aggregate cells.
    *   An aggregate cell is a cell where one of its dimension items has it’s own child items
    *   This includes aggregate cells for line Items with breakback enabled
    *   For a line Item that is dimensioned by a composite list, the only writeable cells are the child items, that are defined directly in the dimension list and not defined in a parent list.
*   In the future, use of this call may be rate limited.

byId.summary: Identify line item and dimension items by ID

byName.summary: Identify line item and dimension items by name/code

get.summary: Retrieve all line item metadata in a module
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

**Note:**This is an early access endpoint and is not generally available yet.

Use this call when you want to get all line items and metadata in a module.

200.description: Line items and metadata in the module.

get.summary: Retrieve IDs and names for views in a module
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve all view IDs and names for a specified module. This call also returns the default view for the module. If the user adds the query parameter includesubsidiaryviews=true, unsaved subsidiary views will also be included in the results.

**Notes**:

*   For the default view, the `{viewId}` is the same as the `{moduleId}`
*   Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response
*   For unsaved subsidiary views, the `{viewId}` and `{viewName}` are the same as the `{lineItemId}` and `{lineItemName}` that the subsidiary view is created from

200.description: IDs and names of views in the module.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

get.summary: List the process definitions available in a model as a JSON array
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Model-direct form of `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/processes`. Returns the same response shape without requiring a workspace ID. Lists process definitions available for the specified model.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort results. `+field` or unprefixed ascending, `-field` descending; comma-separate for multiple columns. `name` and `id` reorder results. Field name not validated — unknown fields return 200 and fall back to default order. Example: `+name`, `-id`.

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve the names and actions for a given Process in an Anaplan model.

**Notes**:

*   Actions which are imports or exports (`actionType` field is `IMPORT` or `EXPORT`) contain additional fields in the response (see below). Other actions just contain the `id`, `name`, and `actionType`.
*   Actions are ordered in the sequence that they run when the Process itself is executed.
*   In the future, use of this call may be rate limited.
*   For an action with `actionType` of `IMPORT`, you can use the `importDataSourceId` value as the ID of a file for the `fileId` parameter when you upload or download files
*   For actions with `actionType` of `EXPORT` or `IMPORT`, you can find more information about them if you use the IDs as `exportId` or `importId` in the export and import metadata endpoints.
*   The possible values for actionType (non-exhaustive list):
    *   IMPORT
    *   EXPORT
    *   DELETE_BY_SELECTION
    *   ORDER_HIERARCHY
    *   OPTIMIZER
*   To include additional information regarding the import data source in an import action, add the `?showImportDataSource=true` query parameter to the request.

parameters.name: showImportDataSource
parameters.description: When set to "true", includes additional information about the import data source. Any value other than "true" is interpreted as false.

get.description:
Minimum role: Workspace Administrator.

Use this call to get information about the model versions. The response contains a list of the versions that the user has access to, and the model metadata that can be viewed from the UI.

**Notes:**

*   The returned versions only include the user’s model role with read access or higher.
*   If the value for a field is set, it will be returned in the results.

put.description:
Minimum role: Workspace Administrator. Use this call to set the switchover date for a version. The response contains the response code, as well as the date and human-readable date period as shown in the UI. **Notes:** * To apply a switchover date, ensure the `versionID` corresponds to a version in Anaplan that is set to either `forecast` or `variance`. * Ensure the switchover date is after the existing version switchover date. * To reset the switchover date to Blank, pass an empty string in the request body. * In the future, use of this call may be rate limited. ### Request body | **JSON element** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `date` |

* Required
* Description: An ISO-formatted (YYYY-MM-DD) date string is used to set the switchover date. An empty string resets the switchover date.
Dates earlier than the current switchover date (for example, `2021-01-01` when the current switchover is `2021-06-01`) or dates outside the current model year return a `400 Bad Request` response.
* Example: `2021-06-01`

|

200.description: The version switchover date as set.

get.summary: Model-direct form: Retrieve IDs and names for all views in a model
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Alternate URL form — response is equivalent to `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/views`.

Use this call to retrieve the ID and name of all views for a model. The results include default and saved views. If you add the query parameter `includesubsidiaryviews=true`, this includes unsaved subsidiary views in the results. This call also retrieves the ID of the module that the views belong to.

**Notes**:

*   Single quotes enclose the module and view names that have special characters
*   Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response

### Default views

For default views:

*   The value of the {viewId} is identical to the value of the {moduleId}
*   The value of the name is identical to the value of the module

### Saved views

For saved views:

*   The `name` consists of the module name, a period, and the saved view name. For example, `REP01 Profit & Loss Report.P & L by Country`
    

For unsaved subsidiary views:

*   The name consists of the module name and the line item name that the subsidiary view is created from
*   These are separated by a period(.)

parameters.name: includesubsidiaryviews
parameters.description: When set to "true", includes unsaved subsidiary views in the results. Any value other than "true" is interpreted as false.

200.description: IDs and names of all views in the model.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

get.summary: Retrieve metadata for dimensions on a view
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve the name, IDs, and lists of names for the dimensions (columns, pages, rows) on a specified view.

**Notes**:

*   If a view has no dimensions on an axis, the call omits the axis field (for example, no `“rows”` field at all instead of `“rows”: []`)
*   If a view is the default view, its `name` is set to be the same as the module name
*   Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response
*   The `viewId` in this request can also be replaced by any valid `lineItemId`. If the Line Item has a changed dimensionality (has a Subsidiary View), the response returns the metadata applicable to that line item. Otherwise, the response returns the default metadata
    

You can use this API call to [map dimensions to cell data export headers](https://help.anaplan.com/43cd0223-edaa-4289-9833-eb9b73666aac-Map-dimensions-to-cell-data-export-headers) or [transform grid CSV format to tabular single column format](https://help.anaplan.com/c8a665b9-6b23-4254-ac68-816657a6e878-Retrieve-the-view-metadata-and-grid-view-data).

200.description: Dimension metadata (columns, pages, rows) for the view.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

get.summary: Retrieve cell data for a view
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve the cell data for a view. You can either query for the default page, or provide page selectors to query for other pages. This call returns data in either a CSV grid response or as JSON.

**Notes:**

*   This call only supports the retrieval of cells from a view that contains a maximum of 1,000,000 cells. If the specified view contains more than 1,000,000 cells, then the call returns a `400 Bad Request` HTTP status, instead of a subset of the cells. Use the [quick sum bar](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/New_User_Experience/Use/Worksheets/Grid/Quick-sum-bar.htm) in Anaplan to identify the number of cells in a view.
*   If you call several times in a short amount of time, you may receive a `429` response.
*   You can replace the `viewId` with any valid `lineItemId`. If the line item has a subsidiary view, the response returns the data as seen in that subsidiary view.
*   For line items without a subsidiary view, the response returns data for the default view of the line item.

### CSV responses

The first line of the CSV response contains the values of the page selectors. The response omits this line when there are no dimensions on pages.

When there are multiple dimensions on columns, a line for each dimension follows the first line that contains the values of the page selectors.

When there are multiple dimensions on rows, a column displays on the left, before the data, for each dimension on rows.

### JSON responses

To receive a JSON-formatted response instead of a CSV-formatted response:

1.  Pass an `Accept` header of `application/json`.
2.  Pass a query parameter of `format` set to `v1`.
    

All other parameters are the same as a CSV-formatted response.

The response returns `Content-Type` `application/json` with this format:

*   A `pages` key with a value that is a list of the selected items for each page selector. If no page selectors exist for this view, the list displays as empty or absent.
*   A `columnCoordinates` key with a value that is a list of the coordinates for all values in that column. Each list item is itself a list containing one or more values. The length of each list equals the number of dimensions on columns.
*   A `rows` key with a value that is a list of row objects. Each row object contains:
    *   A `rowCoordinates` key with a value that is a list of the coordinates for all values in the current row. Each list item is itself a list that contains one or more values. The length of each list equals the number of dimensions on rows.
    *   A `cells` key with a value, which is a list of all the cell values in the current row.

To get the full coordinates for each cell, use the position of the cell value in the `cells` list, and find the column coordinates at the same position in the `columnCoordinates` list. Then, to uniquely specify the cell, combine the column coordinates with the `rowCoordinates` for the current row and any selected pages.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Response format. `text/csv` (default) returns a CSV grid; `application/json` returns structured cell data (also requires query parameter format=v1). Append `;escaped=true` to quote sensitive CSV fields; some tenants block plain text/csv for security and require the escaped form.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Set to `v1` together with Accept: application/json to receive JSON. Ignored for CSV responses.

parameters.name: pages
parameters.description: Page selector values, each in the form `dimensionId:itemId`, choosing which item to display for each page-axis dimension. The page-axis `dimensionId` values come from the `pages` array of `GET /models/{modelId}/views/{viewId}`; the `itemId` values for a given dimension come from `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items`. Both repeated-key form (`pages=a:b&pages=c:d`) and comma-separated form (`pages=a:b,c:d`) are accepted.

200.description: View cell data. Returns text/csv by default; returns application/json when both Accept: application/json and format=v1 are set.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

get.summary: Retrieve selected items in a dimension
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve selected IDs, codes, and names for items in a specified dimension. This call returns data as filtered by the page builder when they configure the view. This call respects hidden items, filtering selections, and [Selective Access](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Users/Selective_Access.html). If the view contains hidden or filtered items, these do not display in the response.

**Notes**:

*   This call works with any dimension type
*   The items in the response may not be ordered
*   The response returns Items within a [flat list](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Dimensions/Lists.html) (no hierarchy)
*   This is a view-level call. For the model-level call, see Retrieve all data for items in a dimension
*   This call only supports the retrieval of dimension items within a view that contains a maximum of 1,000,000 cells. If the specified view contains more than 1,000,000 dimension items, then the call returns a `400 Bad Request` HTTP status, instead of a subset of the dimension items. Use the [quick sum bar](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/New_User_Experience/Use/Worksheets/Grid/Quick-sum-bar.htm) in Anaplan to identify the number of dimension items in a view
*   Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response
*   The `viewId` in this request can also be replaced by any valid `lineItemId`. If the Line Item has a changed dimensionality (has a Subsidiary View), the response returns the applicable dimension items for the given dimension. Otherwise, the response returns all default applicable dimension items for the given dimension

200.description: Selected items of the dimension as filtered by the view.

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Retrieves all users within the tenant.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort results. `+field` ascending, `-field` descending; prefix required and field name validated (bad or unknown field returns 400). Comma-separate to sort by multiple columns. Sortable fields: firstName, lastName, email, active, lastLoginDate.

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Retrieves your user based on your Anaplan authentication token.

Minimum role: Standard User.

Retrieves information about the specified user.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

Minimum role: Standard User.

Retrieves all workspaces a user has access to within the user's default tenant

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort results. `+field` ascending, `-field` descending; prefix required and field name validated (bad or unknown field returns 400). Comma-separate to sort by multiple columns. Sortable fields: name, sizeAllowance.

200.description: All workspaces the user can access.

get.description: Minimum role: Standard User.

404.description: Workspace not found or caller lacks Workspace Administrator role

get.description:
Minimum role: User Administrator.

Returns all administrators for a workspace. Requires tenant admin privilege; workspace admin access alone is insufficient.

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Deletes multiple models in a workspace in a single request. Models that cannot be deleted (e.g., because they are currently open) are listed in `bulkDeleteModelsFailures`; the overall HTTP status is still 200 in that case.

modelIdsToDelete.description: IDs of the models to delete.

200.description: Bulk delete completed. Check `modelsDeleted` and `bulkDeleteModelsFailures` for per-model outcomes.

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Returns models accessible to the calling user within the specified workspace. The response shape is identical to `GET /2/0/models` but filtered to models within the given workspace.

200.description: Models in the workspace accessible to the user.

get.summary: Get a list of action tasks
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order for the task list. Format: [prefix]field where prefix is - (descending), + (ascending), or omitted (ascending). Documented sortable fields: creationDate, taskId, taskState, progress. Example: -creationDate (newest first), +taskId, taskState. The API does not validate the field name — unknown fields are silently accepted and appear to fall back to default ordering.

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

You can POST a deletion task to start the delete. This returns a new `taskID`. Once you have started the delete, you can use GET to receive a list of the deletion tasks as a JSON array. You can use this to monitor the status of the delete.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

get.summary: Get the status of an action task
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Returns the current state of a single action task. Poll this endpoint after starting an action via POST to monitor progress.

Possible `taskState` values: `NOT_STARTED`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `COMPLETE`, `CANCELLING`, `CANCELLED`.

Minimum role: Standard User.

Returns all dashboards defined in the specified model.

get.summary: Get a list of export tasks
get.description: Minimum role: Standard User.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order for the task list. Format: [prefix]field where prefix is - (descending), + (ascending), or omitted (ascending). Documented sortable fields: creationDate, taskId, taskState, progress. Example: -creationDate (newest first), +taskId, taskState. The API does not validate the field name — unknown fields are silently accepted and appear to fall back to default ordering.

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

You can POST an export task to start the export. This returns a new `taskID`. Once you have started the export, you can use GET to receive a list of the export tasks as a JSON array. You can use this to monitor the status of the export tasks.

### Run the export

This creates a task with the specified exportID and returns the newly-created taskID.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

You can get the status of a specific export task. In the response:

*   `currentStep` is the task associated with the specified `taskID`.
*   `taskState` is the status of that export task.
*   `progress` is a double that is typically 1.0.
*   `result` is a JSON object that indicates the object ID, and whether the task succeeded.
*   `taskId` is the ID that you specified in the request.
    

Here is a list of each possible `taskState`:

| **Status** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| NOT_STARTED | The task is scheduled to run, but has not started yet. |
| IN_PROGRESS | The task is currently running. |
| COMPLETE | The task has finished running whether successful or not. |
| CANCELLING | The task is currently being canceled. Cancellation is not yet complete. |
| CANCELLED | The task has been canceled and the changes have been rolled back. |

If failures occur during the export task `details` contains a JSON array that contains debug information

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

Minimum role: Standard User.

Returns metadata for a specific file in the model. Requires Workspace Administrator access; standard Integration API users receive 404. Individual file metadata is also accessible via the full file list endpoint. See integration/README.md for details.

404.description: File not found or endpoint not available for this account role.

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

To upload a file, we recommend setting the `chunkcount` metadata of an upload so that the server knows how many chunks to process during an upload. Set the `chunkcount` to -1 if you do not know how many chunks there will be and will be marking the upload complete when all chunks have been uploaded. If you want to upload the file in a single chunk, see Upload file as a single chunk.

Use chunking:

*   If you want to be able to resume the transfer after the connection is lost during an upload.
*   If you are extracting data from a database and want to push it to the server without holding all the results in memory.
    

To avoid consuming large amounts of memory on the server, a chunk is expected to be between 1 and 50 MB. The chunk count might change if the file to upload has become larger or smaller than the server's version of that file:

*   `id` is the ID of the file for which the chunk count is to be updated.
*   `name` is the filename of the file.
*   `chunkCount` is the number of chunks in the file.
*   `delimiter` is double-quotes for columns and fields. For example, "Versions","Opportunities"," "Version 1","Opportunity 0001".
*   `encoding` determines the way in which bytes in a file map to Unicode character code points. See [Common Character Encodings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Character_encoding#Common_character_encodings) on Wikipedia.
*   `firstDataRow` is the first row after the row containing column names.
*   `format` is text, as opposed to a binary stream.
*   `headerRow` is the row that contains column names.
*   `separator` is the token that separates fields. This is typically a comma, for example, "Versions","Opportunities"," "Version 1","Opportunity 0001".

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

200.description: The file metadata with the chunk count set.

put.summary: Upload file as a single chunk
put.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

You can upload a single file to the server.

**Note:** You cannot compress a file that you are uploading as a single file. If the file is smaller than 1 MB, we recommend you PUT it onto the server by streaming it as an octet stream.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/octet-stream`

### Parameters

#

requestBody.description: Raw binary content of the file.

204.description: File uploaded successfully. No response body.

delete.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

You can delete files that you have uploaded using the API.

**Note:** This only removes private content. Default content and the import data source model object remain.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

204.description: File deleted successfully. No response body.

get.summary: Get the data in a chunk
get.description: Minimum role: Standard User.

requestBody.description: Raw binary content of the chunk.

204.description: Chunk uploaded successfully. No response body.

200.description: Chunk uploaded successfully (some regions may return 200).

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

After carrying out an upload with `chunkCount` set to -1, mark it complete.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

get.summary: Get a list of the import tasks
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

*   Request

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order for the task list. Format: [prefix]field where prefix is - (descending), + (ascending), or omitted (ascending). Documented sortable fields: creationDate, taskId, taskState, progress. Example: -creationDate (newest first), +taskId, taskState. The API does not validate the field name — unknown fields are silently accepted and appear to fall back to default ordering.

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User. You start the import using the POST command. This also receives the task ID value as a JSON object. Once you have started the import, you can use GET to receive a list of the import tasks as a JSON array. You can use this to monitor the status of the import tasks. **Note:** If GET does not return any tasks, the import is not running or might have completed. Tasks sort in ascending order of creation time, with the newest task at the top of the list. To sort in descending order, append `?sort =- creationTime` to the URL. ### Run the import * Request ### Request Body | **Body** | **Description** | | --- | --- | | `{"localeName": "en_US"}` |

* Required
* Description: Specifies the language to use for task status and error messages. `en_US` is the only supported language type.

| #

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

In the response:

*   `currentStep` is the task associated with the specified `taskID`.
*   `taskState` is the status that import task.
*   `progress` is a double that is typically 1.0.
*   `result` is a JSON object that indicates whether a failure dump file was created, the object ID, and whether the task succeeded.
*   `taskID` is the ID that you specified in the request.
    

Here is a list of each possible `taskState`:

| **Status** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| `NOT_STARTED` | The task is scheduled to run, but has not started yet. |
| `IN_PROGRESS` | The task is currently running. |
| `COMPLETE` | The task has finished running whether successful or not. |
| `CANCELLING` | The task is currently being canceled. Cancellation is not yet complete. |
| `CANCELLED` | The task has been canceled and the changes have been rolled back. |

If failures occur during the import task:

*   `details` contains a JSON array that contains debug information
*   If a dump file is available, `failureDumpAvailable` is true and `objectId` contains the ID of the dump file. The dump file contains details of the failures that occurred during the import. You can use the `objectId` to get the dump file.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

Minimum role: Standard User.

If data could not be imported for an import task, a 'dump' file is created depending on the failure. This contains details of the failures that occurred. Use this information to fix the problems and then try the import again.

**Note:** To prevent timeouts on large files, download the dump file in chunks. It is possible to download dump files without chunking, but it is strongly recommended that chunking is used instead.

1.  Query the number of chunks in a dump file.  
    
2.  Download the chunks sequentially.  
    
3.  Concatenate the chunks on your host.
    

Dump files are:

*   Removed 48 hours after the last access of the file or the next time an import task successfully executes with no errors.
*   Maintained on a user-by-user basis and overwritten when the user performs the same import.
    

When an import task has errors, the API maintains a dump file for the task ID with the error.  Any earlier task IDs are erased, though `failureDumpAvailable` can be True for those task IDs.  If a dump file is removed or replaced, then attempting to download the dump file via the API results in a 404 (Not found) HTTP status.

If you use `/tasks?sort=-creationDate` in the URL, it is possible for tasks created by other users to appear first for which the dump file is unavailable to the accessing user.  It is also possible that a task that the accessing user ran, and for which a dump file is still available, to be further down on the same list.

**Note:** The `taskID` is the last task of the user who carried out the specified import.

200.description: Full dump file content (unchunked). Plain text with CSV-formatted error rows. Prefer /dump/chunks for large files to avoid timeouts.

204.description: No dump content available (import completed with no failures).

404.description: Dump file not found, not yet available, or has expired (48-hour TTL after last access).

get.summary: Query the number of chunks in a dump file
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Begin by querying the number of chunks using the `/chunks` endpoint.

It is possible for a dump file to be very large. To help manage the size of a dump file and prevent timeouts, download the dump file in chunks. Chunks are 10mb or less and numbered sequentially from 0.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

200.description: List of chunks in the import failure dump file.

404.description: Dump file not found or not yet available for this task.

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Once you have the chunk count, download them sequentially and concatenate. Download each chunk by its `id` (from the `.../dump/chunks` list).

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

200.description: Dump file chunk content. Plain text with CSV-formatted error rows; each row contains _Status_, data columns, _Line_, and _Error_1_ fields.

404.description: Chunk not found or dump has expired.

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to retrieve list data for the items in a specified list.

You can filter on optional fields by setting the `includeAll` query parameter.

**Notes:**

*   Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response.
    

The following list data always displays in the query response:

| **Default List Data** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| `id` | The Anaplan ID of the item in the list. |
| `name` | The name of the item in the list. |
| `code` | The code value for an item in the list (if specified) |
| `parent` | The name of the parent item in the list. |
| `parentId` | The Anaplan ID of the parent item in the list. |
| `listId` | The Anaplan ID of the list to which the parent item belongs. This ID displays if the list has a **Parent Hierarchy** set under the **Configure** tab in Anaplan. |
| `listName` | The name of list to which the parent item belongs. The name displays if the list has a **Parent Hierarchy** set under the **Configure** tab in Anaplan. |

Set the `includeAll` query parameter to true to display the following optional list data fields in the response.

| **Optional List Data** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| `write` | If [Selective Access](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Users/Selective_Access.html) is enabled for the list, you can add individual users to provide `write` access. Users with `write` access can view and edit data associated with the list items. |
| `read` | If [Selective Access](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Users/Selective_Access.html) is enabled for the list, you can add individual users to provide `read` access. Users with `read` access can view the data associated with the list items but not edit it. |
| `subsets` | If the value of the subset column is true, the associated item belongs to that subset.  You can view subsets under **Subsets** tab in Anaplan. |
| `properties` | The value of each custom list property. |

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests. Retry after a delay.

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User. Use this call to add items to a list. You must provide the list ID and data for each item. You can add one or more items in each request. **Notes:** * A maximum of 100,000 list items can be added with a single call. ### Request body | **List Data** | **Description** | | --- | --- | | `{name}` | The name value for the item in the list. | | `{code}` | The code value for the item in the list. | | `{parent}` | The name of the parent item in the list | | `{properties}` | The value of each custom list property. | | `{subsets}` | The value of each custom list subset. | ### Logic for add items The add list items behavior uses specific logic with names and codes. This logic varies between [general lists](https://help.anaplan.com/f7f01453-71bc-4046-935a-b9d7cb47d15a-General-Lists) and [numbered lists](https://help.anaplan.com/54b75174-848f-482b-a051-a9a063ece50c-Numbered-Lists) in Anaplan. ### Add items to a general list | **Scenario** | **Existing** | **Input in API** | **Behavior** | **Result** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Add a new list Item | There are no existing items | name=n1
code=c1 | Success.
A new list item is created. | name=n1
code=c1 | | Add list item with name conflicts | name=n1
code=c1 | name=n1
code=c2 | There is a duplicate name. A new list item is not added. | An error message returns. | | Add list item with code conflicts | name=n1
code=c1 | name=n2
code=c1 | There is a duplicate name. A new list item is not added. | An error message returns. | | Add list item without name | There are no existing items | code=c1 | There is a missing name. A new list item is not added. | An error message returns. | ### Add items to a numbered list For a numbered list, the name column is used for the index. The name is automatically generated. When you add an item, the API does not allow a name as an input. | **Scenario** | **Existing** | **Input in API** | **Behavior** | **Result** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Add a new list Item | There are no existing items | name=n1
code=c1 | A name is not a valid input. A new list item is not added. | An error message returns. | | Add a new list item | There are no existing items | code=c1 | Success.
A new item is added. | success code=c1 | | Add a list item with a conflicting code | code=c1 | code=c1 | There is a duplicate code. A new list item is not added. | An error message returns. |

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation to perform: `add` appends new items, `delete` removes items identified by name, code, or id.

items.description: Array of items to add or delete.
A list item. For add: provide name/code/parent/properties/subsets. For delete: identify by name, code, or id.

name.description: Item display name. Required for named lists when adding; used as identifier when deleting.

code.description: Item code. Used as primary identifier for numbered lists.

id.description: Item ID. Returned by GET list items; can be used as identifier when deleting.

parent.description: Parent item name for hierarchical lists.

properties.description: Custom list property values keyed by property name.

subsets.description: Subset membership flags keyed by subset name.

200.description: Operation completed; per-item failures reported inline

put.description:
Minimum role: Standard User. Use this call to update items to a list. You must provide the model ID and list ID on the request URL. There are two kinds of lists in Anaplan: standard list and numbered list. For a standard list, the API identifies the item using the id, code, or name. For numbered list, the API uses the id or code. If property or subset is not specified in the input, the existing value is not updated. You can update one or more items in each request. When you specify the list item identifier, select one of the appropriate identifiers (id, code, or name). If the request contains multiple identifiers, the API returns an error. **Notes:** * A maximum of 100,000 list items can be updated with a single call. Note: When an update to a list item completes successfully, all downstream calculations are also complete. This includes cells linked by formulas to the cells being updated. ### Request body | **List Data** | **Description** | | --- | --- | | `{name}` | The name value for the item in the list. | | `{code}` | The code value for the item in the list. | | `{id}` | The Anaplan ID of the item in the list. | | `{parent}` | The name of the parent item in the list | | `{properties}` | The value of each custom list property. | | `{subsets}` | The value of each custom list subset. | ### Update logic The update list item endpoint logic varies by the update scenario. | **Scenario** | **Existing** | **Input in API** | **Behavior** | **Result** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Update list item | name=n1
code=c1 | name=n1
code=c1 | Success | name=n1
code=c1 | | Update name | name=n1
code=c1 | name=n2
code=c1 | Success | name=n2
code=c1 | | Update code | id=id1
name=n1
code=c1 | id=id1
name=n1
code=c2 | Success | id=id1
name=n1
code=c2 | | Update code without existing code | id=id1
name=n1 | id=id1
name=n1
code=c2 | Success | id=id1
name=n1
code=c2 | | Update code
(removing existing code) | id=id1
name=n1
code=c1 | id=id1
name=n1
code="" | This scenario does not provide a new code to replace the existing value. An error returns. | error: `INCOMPLETE_DATA` | | Update code without an existing code
(assign a code without the id) | name=n1 | name=n1
code=c1 | Success | name=n1
code=c1 | | Update code with same name | name=n1
code=c1 | name=n1
code=c2 | The name is already used. An error returns. | error: `INCOMPLETE_DATA` | | Update value with code only | name=n1
code=c1
properties{p1=1234} | code=c1
properties{p1=1234} | Success
It's valid to use the code to identify the record. | code=c1
properties {p1=4321} | | Update value with name only.
Do not specify the code | name=n1
code=c1
properties{p1=1234} | name=n1
properties{p1=1234} | The code is required to identify the record. An error returns. | error: `INCOMPLETE_DATA` | | Update value with name only | name=n1
properties{p1=1234} | name=n1
properties{p1=4321} | Success
When the code is not defined, the API can still update with the name. | name=n1
properties{p1=4321} | ### Response 200 body The response body contains a JSON object that contains `total` (total number of items in request), `updated` (number of items updated), `ignored` (number of items ignored), and `failures` fields. On `failures`, each field contains information about any errors encountered while performing the update action. `requestIndex` is the index in the input items array.

200.description: List items updated; per-item failures reported inline

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Initiates an asynchronous read of all items in the specified list. Poll the returned `url` until `requestState` is COMPLETE, then retrieve pages via the pages sub-resource.

get.summary: Get the list of tasks for the process
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

This lists the tasks in the process for the given processID. This returns a JSON array containing the list of tasks in the process definition.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order for the task list. Format: [prefix]field where prefix is - (descending), + (ascending), or omitted (ascending). Documented sortable fields: creationDate, taskId, taskState, progress. Example: -creationDate (newest first), +taskId, taskState. The API does not validate the field name — unknown fields are silently accepted and appear to fall back to default ordering.

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

You can POST a process task to run a process. This returns a new `taskID`. Once you have started the process, you can use GET to receive a list of tasks as a JSON array. You can use this to monitor the status of each task.

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

In the response:

*   `currentStep` is the task associated with the specified `taskID`.
*   `taskState` is the status of the process task.
*   `progress` is a double that is typically 1.0.
*   `result` is a JSON object that indicates whether a failure dump file was created, the `objectID`, and whether the task succeeded.
*   `failureDumpAvailable` indicates whether this process container or an action within it has a failure dump file. **Note:** For a deletion action, `failureDumpAvailable` is always false.
*   `nestedResults` indicates whether the actions carried out in the process were successful. This contains details in the form of a JSON array, which also contains some debug information when failures occur. Get the failure dump file to view the complete debug information.
*   `objectID` is the identifier of the process task or a task that it includes. For instance, if `nestedResults` has `failureDumpAvailable` true, you need the `objectID` within that section to obtain the related 'dump' file.
*   `taskID` is the ID that you specified in the request.
    

Here is a list of each possible `taskState`:

| **Status** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| NOT_STARTED | The task is scheduled to run, but has not started yet. |
| IN_PROGRESS | The task is currently running. |
| COMPLETE | The task has finished running whether successful or not. |
| CANCELLING | The task is currently being canceled. Cancellation is not yet complete. |
| CANCELLED | The task has been canceled and the changes have been rolled back. |

### Headers

*   `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}`
*   `Content-Type:application/json`

### Parameters

#

post.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to initiate a large volume read request of view data. This enables you to read data from views that are larger than a million cell count.

**Note:**

*   As a best practice, use the Delete read requests call to clear all pages from completed exports as soon as you download all pages. Doing so will make space available for future exports.

### Request Body

This API supports exporting data in the following formats:

| **Type** | **Description** | **More information** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GRID_ALL_PAGES` | This layout respects the existing sorting and filtering you have imposed on a data grid before you launch the export. | [Export grid](https://help.anaplan.com/Import_and_Export/Export_Layouts/Export_Data_in_Grid_Layout.html) |
| `TABULAR_SINGLE_ITEMS` | This layout takes all dimensions and puts them in columns, with the final column reserved for data. | [Export tabular single column](https://help.anaplan.com/Import_and_Export/Export_Layouts/Export_Data_in_Tabular_Single_Column_Layout.html) |
| `TABULAR_MULTI_COLUMN` | This layout enables you to export multiple data columns. If you set up a Boolean line item, you can use it as a filter to control which data is exported. This is especially useful when exporting from large modules. It saves the manual task of marking data cells for export. | [Export Tabular Multiple Column](https://help.anaplan.com/Import_and_Export/Export_Layouts/Export_Data_in_Tabular_Multiple_Column_Layout.html) |

200.description: The created read request, including the URL to poll for status.

get.summary: Retrieve status of large read request
get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to check the status of an export action after a bulk data long request is initiated. You can use it to check how many pages are available to download.

### Response scenarios

*   When `requestState` is `COMPLETE` and `successful` is `true` , the export has completed successfully and the user can download all the pages.
*   When `requestState` is `IN_PROGRESS` and `successful` is `false`, the export has not yet completed. You can download the available pages indicated by the `availablePages` field.
*   When `requestState` is `COMPLETE` and `successful` is `false`, this means the export failed. Retry and initiate a new export.
*   When 'requestState' is 'NOT_STARTED' then simply check the status again.
*   When requestState' is 'CANCELLED' then this request is completed by a 'DELETE' call.
    

We recommend you delete the last failed export before initiating a new export, using the delete export endpoint.

200.description: Status of the read request, including available page count.

delete.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to delete or cancel an initiated read request. This removes all the pages from the file store and stops the ongoing read request.

**Notes**:

*   As a best practice, use this call to clear all pages from completed exports as soon as you download all pages. Doing so will make space available for future exports.
*   The system will automatically reclaim space for read requests not accessed for over 30 minutes. The same applies to failed read requests.

### Response 200 body

200.description: Read request cancelled or removed; pages cleared from file store.

get.description:
Minimum role: Standard User.

Use this call to download the available pages, either when the read request is in progress, or when the request is completed. This request returns a CSV format response of the export list items.

You can download pages while the export is in progress. You can download up to `availablePages` number of pages returned by the export status API.

Note that page numbers start with zero. That means if 10 pages are available, you can download from page=0 through page=9

**Note:**

*   As a best practice, use the **Delete read requests** call to clear all pages from completed exports as soon as you download all pages. Doing so will make space available for future exports.

200.description: One page of view data as CSV.

get.description:
Minimum role: User Administrator.

Returns all visitors for a workspace. Requires tenant admin privilege; workspace admin access alone is insufficient.

CellWrite.description: A request to set the value of a single cell, identified by the line item it belongs to plus one coordinate for each dimension that applies to that line item (including Time and Versions).

lineItemId.description: ID of the line item that belongs to the module. Specify either lineItemId or lineItemName. From `GET /models/{modelId}/modules/{moduleId}/lineItems` (`items[].id`).

lineItemName.description: Name of the line item that belongs to the module. Specify either lineItemId or lineItemName.

dimensions.description: Coordinate for each dimension that applies to the line item. May be omitted when the line item is a single cell with no dimensions.

items.description: A dimension coordinate: the dimension plus the item selected on it.

dimensionId.description: ID of the dimension on the line item. Specify either dimensionId or dimensionName. From `GET /models/{modelId}/lineItems/{lineItemId}/dimensions` (`dimensions[].id`).

dimensionName.description: Name of the dimension on the line item. Specify either dimensionId or dimensionName.

itemId.description: ID of the item selected on the dimension. Specify one of itemId, itemName, or itemCode. From `GET /models/{modelId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items` (`items[].id`).

itemName.description: Name of the item selected on the dimension. Specify one of itemId, itemName, or itemCode.

itemCode.description: Code of the item selected on the dimension. Specify one of itemId, itemName, or itemCode.

value.description: The value to set for the cell. Must be a string, number, or boolean compatible with the line item's format/data type. Cannot be null.

TaskId.name: taskId
TaskId.description: Task identifier, scoped to the parent action/import/export/process that launched it: a taskId is valid only under the same parent, so an action's taskId will not resolve under a process, import, or export. Returned when a run is launched by `POST /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/imports/{importId}/tasks` (or the equivalent actions, exports, or processes tasks endpoint), and listed by the matching `GET .../tasks` — `tasks[].taskId`, except the import tasks response, which nests it under `task.taskId`.

TenantDetails.name: tenantDetails
TenantDetails.description: When `true`, the response includes additional workspace detail fields (`sizeAllowance` and `currentSize`). When `false` or omitted, only `id`, `name`, and `active` are returned.

FileId.name: fileId
FileId.description: File identifier (12-digit numeric, prefix 113). From `GET /models/{modelId}/files`, or the `importDataSourceId` of `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/imports/` — the file an import reads, which is the file you upload before running that import.

ModelId.name: modelId
ModelId.description: Model identifier (32-character uppercase hex string). From `GET /models` or `GET /workspaces/{workspaceId}/models`.

ListId.name: listId
ListId.description: List identifier (12-digit numeric string, prefix 101). From `GET /models/{modelId}/lists`.

UserId.name: userId
UserId.description: User identifier (32-character hex string). From `GET /users`, or `GET /users/me` for the caller's own id.

ModuleId.name: moduleId
ModuleId.description: Module identifier (12-digit numeric string, prefix 102). From `GET /models/{modelId}/modules`.

Search.name: s
Search.description: Optional case-insensitive filter. Matches items whose name or ID contains this string. Supports wildcards `%` (0-n characters) and `_` (exactly 1 character). Omit to return all items. **Undocumented and may behave unpredictably; requires the Tenant Admin role and is ignored for non-admin users.**

ImportId.name: importId
ImportId.description: Import definition identifier (12-digit numeric, prefix 112). From `GET /models/{modelId}/imports`.

WorkspaceId.name: workspaceId
WorkspaceId.description: Workspace identifier (32-char lowercase hex). When the path also has a `modelId`, use that model's `currentWorkspaceId` (`GET /models/{modelId}`, or `GET /models` matched by `name`). For workspace-only endpoints, scan `GET /workspaces` by `name`.

ViewId.name: viewId
ViewId.description: View identifier. From `GET /models/{modelId}/views` or `GET /models/{modelId}/modules/{moduleId}/views`. A line item ID from `GET /models/{modelId}/modules/{moduleId}/lineItems` also works when that line item has a subsidiary view, returning that view's data.

LineItemId.name: lineItemId
LineItemId.description: Line item identifier (12-digit numeric, prefix 206). From `GET /models/{modelId}/lineItems` or `GET /models/{modelId}/modules/{moduleId}/lineItems`.

ExportId.name: exportId
ExportId.description: Export definition identifier (12-digit numeric, prefix 116). From `GET /models/{modelId}/exports`.

ChunkId.name: chunkId
ChunkId.description: The chunk identifier. From the `id` values returned by the sibling `GET .../chunks` list endpoint for the file or dump. Chunk ids are zero-based; chunk `0` is always the first chunk of a file that has any content.

IncludeAll.name: includeAll
IncludeAll.description: When `true`, optional fields are included in the response (for line items, the full metadata field set). When `false` or omitted, only the default fields are returned.

PageNo.name: pageNo
PageNo.description: Zero-based page index of a read request result set.
